text: The lactate-guided threshold interval training (LGTIT) model consists of performing three to four threshold interval sessions and one VO2max intensity session per week within a high-volume low-intensity framework.
specific_value: 3-4 LGTIT sessions + 1 VO2max session per week

text: In the LGTIT model, low-intensity running is performed up to an overall volume of 150-180 km per week alongside the high-intensity sessions.
During lactate-guided threshold interval sessions, training pace is dictated by blood lactate concentration targets typically ranging from 2 to 4.5 mmol/L, measured every one to three repetitions.

Threshold-intensity intervals produce lower central and peripheral fatigue between high-intensity sessions compared to greater intensities, potentially allowing for more rapid recovery and greater weekly volume of quality workouts.
The interval format of threshold training allows achievement of high absolute running speeds and maximal motor unit recruitment despite a relatively low metabolic intensity in the threshold zone.
Lactate-guided threshold interval training may increase mitochondrial proliferation through optimization of both calcium and AMPK signaling pathways.
